title: Secret redaction after log emission
description: Secret values are redacted after they have already reached logs, traces, or artifacts.
body: Failure mode: code logs a full object and relies on downstream processors, dashboards, or support tooling to hide sensitive fields. The raw value may already be stored, indexed, exported, or included in crash reports. Detection signals include logger calls before redaction, exception serialization of config objects, and snapshot tests that mask only presentation layers. Remediation is redaction before the first emission point. Human gate is required because existing logged secrets may need rotation. Use when a proposed approach skips trust boundaries, authorization decisions, dangerous sinks, secrets, and dependency execution or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in negative security tests, server-side checks, sink payload tests, and secret scans. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause client-only controls, injection, credential leakage, permissive fallback, and unsafe dependency changes.
